scope: PDT-2553: When sending email to more than one recipient, deliver one email with multiple recipients in the To field (single message), not one separate email per recipient.
summary: Customer Feedback (Phoenix Delivery). Expected: one outbound message with all intended recipients visible to each other (or at least one SMTP transaction with multiple To). Current behaviour (pre-change): CRM email communication flow iterates contacts and calls EmailSenderService.sendEmail once per address, which builds SendEmailRequest with a single addToRecepient per call — effectively N emails for N contacts.
entry_points: EmailSenderService.sendEmail(String recipientEmailAddress, ...) — mass comm client single recipient per request
EmailSenderServiceInterface — API surface for outbound mail

upstream.name: mass_comm EmailCommunicationClient / SendEmailRequestBuilder
upstream.usage: Builds SendEmailRequest; must support multiple To recipients if product batches addresses into one message.

upstream.name: crm email_communication* tables (EmailCommunicationCustomer, EmailCommunicationCustomerContact)
upstream.usage: Stores contacts per customer; status/taskId per contact today assumes per-contact sends.

downstream.name: Email status polling / EmailCommunicationStatusUpdateJobService
downstream.usage: Fetches task statuses per contact; may need one task per message or mapped multi-recipient semantics.

downstream.name: Email communication UI and listing
downstream.usage: User expects recipient list; verify display matches single-message semantics.

shared: Termination / objection flows use email client with single recipient pattern — regression if interface changes
integration_points: Mass communication email API (send + task status)

what_could_break.reason: Single email with multiple To may return one task for the whole message; contact-level SUCCESS/ERROR mapping must be redesigned or aggregated.
Today one failing recipient does not block others; with one message, bounce behaviour differs.
All To recipients see each other; may need Bcc strategy if privacy is required.
Any new multi-recipient method or signature must be implemented in mock/profile test beans.

technical_details.merge_lookup: No local git commits referencing PDT-2553 in phoenix-core, phoenix-billing-run, or phoenix-payment-api (grep log empty at analysis time).
technical_details.conditional_sync: Skipped — no merge found for key in sampled repos.
technical_details.changed_modules_note: Likely phoenix-core-lib: EmailCommunicationSenderService, EmailSenderService(+Interface), possibly mass_comm client usage; verify MR in GitLab for PDT-2553 for authoritative file list.

jira_snapshot_2026_03_30.summary: When send email to more than one recipient, to send one email with multiple recipients not many separate emails
